evals.prompt: Create a threat model for a password reset flow. The user enters their email, receives a reset link, clicks it, and sets a new password. The link contains a token that's valid for 1 hour.
evals.expected_output: A STRIDE threat model covering: Spoofing (token theft/prediction), Tampering (email address manipulation), Repudiation (no audit of reset requests), Information Disclosure (token in URL), DoS (rate limiting), Elevation of Privilege (account takeover via reset). Should include mitigations for each.
expectations: Document identifies token prediction or brute force as a spoofing/auth threat
Document identifies information disclosure risk from token in URL (logs, Referer)
Document identifies DoS/rate limiting risk on the reset endpoint
Document identifies account takeover as an elevation of privilege risk
Each threat includes at least one mitigation
Document includes a risk summary table

evals.prompt: Threat model a multi-tenant SaaS API where tenants are isolated by a tenant_id field in the database. The API uses JWT tokens that include the tenant_id claim. Service A calls Service B internally.
evals.expected_output: Threat model covering: JWT tampering to change tenant_id (Spoofing/Tampering), IDOR via missing tenant isolation checks (Elevation of Privilege), internal service authentication (Spoofing), tenant data leakage in shared database (Information Disclosure), service-to-service auth.
expectations: Document identifies tenant isolation bypass as a critical threat (IDOR/missing checks)
Document addresses JWT tampering risks (tenant_id in payload)
Document identifies cross-tenant data leakage as an information disclosure risk
Mitigations include tenant ID validation on every DB query
Risk summary identifies tenant isolation bypass as high/critical
